def: n. m. A borer; one who perforates pearls, gems, the human ear, etc.
v.t. To pierce, perforate, bore, make a hole through or in: to string (pearls, beads, &c.); to pierce or wound with sharp speech, to say cutting things to.
more: v. a. S. बेधन from विध् to pierce; Pr. bhindaī he breaks. 1. To perforate; bore; punch; drill; make a hole; pierce; pink; prick. Nainan kī aujhaṛ burī, jūṅ nāvak ko tīr, Sar bin, par bin, bhāl bin bīṅdho sakal sarīr . Dohā. 2. To say hard things; speak bitterly; to taunt. Mujhe to sāsū rāt din bīṅdhe hai .

def: v.t. To puncture, &c. = bīṅdhnā, q.v.; — v.n. (from bīṅdhṉā), To be punctured, be perforated, bored, or pierced; to be stung.
more: v. n. S. भिद् to pierce. To be pierced, perforated, bored. Biṅdh gayā so motī, rah gayā so patthar . Prov. Pearls are bored, stones are left.
v. a. To sting, to bite. v. n. To be bored or pierced.

def: v.t. To cause to be pierced, to have or get pierced, bored, &c.; to pierce, perforate, bore, &c. ( = bīṅdhnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. Caus. of bīṅdhnā , q. v. Sukh kāran sāgar tajo, ān biṅdhāyo aṅg, Motī nar yūṅ kampiyāṅ, haṅsī aur ke saṅg . Dohā. For pleasure ocean's bed I left, my body bored, often, A pearl and man, I tremble so—you laugh with (A pearl in a nose-ring).

def: s.m. = bindhāćal, q.v. s.m. The range of mountains which stretches across India, and divides Hindūstān proper from the Dakkhan, marking the southern boundary of the sacred land of the Hindūs.
s.f. Dot over a letter; dot, point, spot, mark, cypher, nought; — spangle, ornament for the forehead.
more: n. f. S. बिन्दु a drop. 1. A spot; cypher ( sifar ); dot; mark. Ekṛe pe bindī do, gine gināë pūre sau. Mahārnī. Hindī Pāṭshālā . Two cyphers add to one, to one hundred it's grown. 2. A spangle; an ornament for the forehead. Chhīn līyo mukh murlī, pitambar, sir pe chunaryā uṛhāī, Beṅdī bhāl, nain bich kājar, nāk besar pahrāī, Mano naī nār banāī! Holī song. His yellow garments snatched, and from his mouth his pipe, A woman's sheet thrown o'er his head, a spangle on His forehead, painted black his eyes, and through anon His nose a besar , he looked like a maiden ripe!

def: s.f. The hair twisted or plaited behind; braided hair; queuc, tail
more: s. f. The hair twisted or plaited behind; a tail. See بيني beṇī.
subs: v.t. To form the hair into plaits or braids.

def: s.m. A third ox yoked before a pair of draught oxen, or a fifth yoked before two pairs.
s. m. Semen cito emittens morbo affectus.
s.m. Drop; point, dot, spot. mark; the nasal dot anusvāra; semen, sperma genitale
subs: s.m. Semen cito emittens morbo affectus.

def: n. m. A third or fifth ox yoked before a pair of draught oxen.
s.f. Nose, snout; — the part of a door-leaf which overlaps the other; rib of wood fixed on a door-panel to strengthen it; the guard of a sword
more: n. f. 1. The part of the door leaf which overlaps the other. 2. The guard of a sword.
s. f. 1. The nose. بيني بريده bīnīburīda, adj. Nose-clipt. 2. in compos. (from بين bīn ) The act of seeing or regarding.
subs: adj. Having the nose cut, nose-clipt, noseless ( = nakṭā).

def: s.f. The act of seeing or regarding (used in comp.)
s.f. Seeing, sight; discernment.
part. adj. Well-trained, educated, disciplined, well-behaved, well-controlled; compliant, governable, tractable, humble, modest, meek, placid, gentle.
s.m. = bīj, q.v.

s.m. Anything used in dressing or preparing food, seasoning, relish, sauce, condiment; vegetables (dressed with butter, or ghī, and added to flesh or fish); — (in Gram.) a consonant (as marking or distinguishing sound).
more: n. S. व्यञ्जन 1. Vegetables. 2. ( Gram .) A consonant.
s. m. 1. Sauce, condiments, particularly vegetables dressed with butter, &c. and added to flesh or fish. 2. (In grammar) A consonant.

def: v.t. See بننا binnā.
more: v. n. To be picked, cleaned.
v. a. 1. To knit. 2. To weave. 3. v. n. To be picked.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: v.t. See بيننا bīnnā.
more: v. a. To pick; pick out; select; cull.

ety: bīn˚, or bin˚ = Prk. वीण(इ), or विण(इ) = S. व्रीणा(ति), or व्रिणा(ति), rt. व्री
def: v.t. To pick, glean, gather, cull, pluck; to pick out, select.
v.t. To pick up, gather up, &c. ( = bīnnā, q.v.); — v.n. To be picked, culled; to be cleaned.

ety: bin˚ or bun˚ = Prk. विण(इ) or वुण(इ) = S. वृणा(ति) or वृणो(ति), rt. वृ
def: v.t. To weave; to braid, plait, knit; to intertwine, interlace.
s.m. Dismissing removing; dismissal, abandonment; diversion, sport, pastime, play, entertainment, pleasure, gratification, happiness, interest; eagerness, vehemence.
s.m. A small roll of cloth, &c. (see bīṅḍā, and īṅḍwā).
s.m. = bhinsār, q.v. s.m. Dawn of day, day-break, day-light, early morning (also written bhinusār, bhinnsār); — adv. At dawn, &c.

ety: fem. of ابن ibn; rt. بني
def: s.f. Daughter
more: (Heb. ) s.f. A daughter. بنت العنب bintu-l-iﻌnab, Daughter of the grape; wine.
subs: s.f. Daughter of the grape, wine.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To cut out or shape clothes: see بيونتنا byontnā.
s.f. Cost of, or price paid for, weaving, &c.
v.t. To cause to be picked, to have or get (a thing) picked, &c.

ety: caus. of binnā or bunnā 'to weave'
def: v.t. To cause to be woven, &c.; to get (a thing) woven, &c.
more: v. a. Caus. of بيّنا q. v.

def: Moment, instant, second.
more: n. m. S. पल् to move. ( Ast .) A second.

def: s.f. Opposition, contrariety, contrary declaration; hostility, disaffection, mischief, ruin
more: adj. S. विपरीत from वि against, परि + इ to turn. 1. Hostile; antagonistic; inimical; unfriendly. 2. Opposite; counter; contrasted; against ( khilāf ). 3. Informal; irregular; contrary; wrong. biprīt , adv. In opposition; versus ; against.

n. f. 1. Disaffection; hostility; enmity. 2. Opposition; contradiction; contrary declaration. biprīt āgyā denā , v. a. To overrule an order; upset a decision. biprīt-budh , n. f. Misconception. biprit barnan karnā , v. a. To misrepresent; make a false statement. biprīt samajh'nā , v. n. To misapprehend; misconceive; misunderstand; mistake. biprīt karnā , v. a. To pervert; turn aside; go against; violate. biprīt lekh , n. m. Misquotation; wrong citation; unfounded assertion.
part. Contrary, opposite; s. f. Mischief, ruin.
subs: To reverse an order or decision
To misrepresent; to make a false statement
(-ke), To pervert; to turn away or aside, to be hostile; to violate

def: v.n. = bipharnā, q.v. v.n. To struggle (against, -se), resist; to disagree, jar, clash; to become vexed, irritated or enraged (with, -se), to break into a passion (syn. ćiṛnā); to break loose from control. to revolt, rise (against), make an attack (upon); to turn wild and wanton, run riot; to get into disorder, run to ruin; to be perverse, refractory. obstinate, &c. ( = ḍhīṭh honā); to insist, persist (in).
more: v. a. To attack, to assault.

def: s.m. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, trouble, disaster, mishap; distress, pain, suffering
more: s. f. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, distress.
subs: (-par), Calamity or misfortune befalling, to be overtaken by calamity, &c.

def: s.m. Thursday ( = bihphai, q.v.). s.m. Thursday.

v.n. To struggle (against, -se), resist; to disagree, jar, clash; to become vexed, irritated or enraged (with, -se), to break into a passion (syn. ćiṛnā); to break loose from control. to revolt, rise (against), make an attack (upon); to turn wild and wanton, run riot; to get into disorder, run to ruin; to be perverse, refractory. obstinate, &c. ( = ḍhīṭh honā); to insist, persist (in).
more: v. n. S. विपरीत, , H. bi against, phirnā to turn. 1. To disagree; clash; jar. 2. To be perverse, refractory, obstinate. 3. To be disobedient, undutiful; to disobey; revolt. Fauj biphar gaī . 4. To insist; persist ( machalnā ). 5. To be transported with rage; to be roused; to flare up. Biphre rizāle aur bhūke ashrāf se ḍarye . Prov. The angry low and the hungry high are alike to be feared. 6. To be ready to fight; take up arms.
(perhaps from विपरीत ) v. n. To be perverse, refractory, disobedient, cross, obstinate, pert.

def: s.m. A Brāhmaṇ, priest, poet or singer of Vedic hymns.
more: n. m. S. विप्र A Brāhman. Pūjnīë hai bipr sadāī, Guni, auguni ho kaiso, bhāī . Bihāri Lāl. The Brahman to be worshipped is, Or good or bad, whate'er's amiss.

def: s.m. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, trouble, disaster, mishap; distress, pain, suffering
more: n. f. S. पत् 1. Misfortune. 2. Trouble. See اَفت 3. Distress; suffering; pain ( dukh ). 1. Jahāṅ su-mati, tahāṅ sampat nānā, Jahāṅ ku-mati, tahāṅ bipat nidānā . Rāmāyan. Where'er good sense and peace prevail, there wealth of sorts you find, Where judgments false prevail, there sufferings are of every kind. 2. Ban hī meṅ jāëb, ban phal khāëb, ban hī meṅ biptā gaṅwāeb, māī . Bhoj. Song. To the forest I would go, on its wild herbs live, in suffering pass my days, my mother. 3. Prem-agan nit mohe jarāve, vākā bhed kahūṅ kä se? Pī ho pās, to jī ho ṭhanḍā, apnī biptā kahūṅ vā se . Wom. Zafar. In Love's flames I am scorched, to whom my grievous pain declare? My own love near, my heart relieved, I could my bosom bare. 4. Load; pressure; reverse; burden. 5. Blast; blow; blight; plague. Phr.—A bitter pill; a thorn in one's side. biptā paṛnā , v. n. To befall (misfortune); to be overtaken by calami…
s. f. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, distress.
subs: (-par), Calamity or misfortune befalling, to be overtaken by calamity, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Heroic, brave. mighty, powerful; — hero, brave man, warrior, champion (syn. bahādur); brother; a class of spirits employed by a magician to injure or molest anyone; supernatural power, spell, charm; a jewel worn in the ear; — s.f. Sister; tusk; pasturage, pasture (local)
more: n. m. Pr. viriam heroism. 1. A hero ( bahādur ). 2. ( bīran ) A brother. 1. Maryo, rī sās, terā bīr, kanth merā taiṅ marvāyā, Ḍhāī baras mere biyāh ko bīte, chālā karvāyā . Play. Raghbīr Singh. Die, mother-in-law, your brother, for my husband you have slain, But thirty months we married were, you've put him out amain. 2. Kāṭūn apnā sir, bīr bin kiskī kasmāṅ khāūṅgī, Kiskī gālī deṅgī sāthan, jāṅ kahīṅ khelan jāūṅgī? Rus. Song. My head I will cut off, no brother on whose head to swear, By whose name can playmates abuse when in their sports I share? 3. Charm; spell; supernatural power. bīr , adj. Heroic; gallant; chivalrous ( bahādur ). Phr. Bold as a lion; brave to the backbone. bīr tā'ī, bīrtā , n. f. Heroism ( bahādurī ).
1. s. m. A hero. 2. adj. Brave.
1. s. m. A brother. 2. A jewel worn in the ear. 3. s. f. A tusk.
subs: s.f. A man's own wife (used in North-Western India, particularly among the Jāṭs)
s.f. A silver or gold chain worn by women on the forehead

bīr-bahūṭī, bīr-bahoṭī [S. vīra+vadhuṭī], s.f. The red-velvet insect, the scarlet or lady-fly, Buccella carniola (commonly called the "rain-insect," as it makes its appearance when the first rains have fallen: it is covered with a downy exterior resembling velvet, and of a scarlet colour
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: s.m. Power, strength; seed, &c. ( = bīj, q.v.).
more: s. m. 1. See بيج bīj. 2. Power, strength.

def: s.m. Well, pit.
s.m. = bīṛā, q.v.
more: n. m. E. bīrī , n. f. S. बीड़ा 1. A betel leaf, folded generally in a triangular form, with spices, areca nut, and cardamum. It is much used by the natives of all parts of India and is commonly presented by one to another in token of civility or affection. It is also given in confirmation of a pledge, promise, or betrothal, and, among the Rājpūts, is sometimes exchanged as a challenge. 2. A thong tied to the hilt of a sword by which it is retained in the scabbard; a sword-knot; the hilt of a sword. 3. A quid of tobacco; a cheroot or cigar. bīṛā uṭhā'nā , v. a. To take up the gauntlet; take upon oneself any enterprize, or the destruction or slaughter of another. bīṛā ḍāl'nā , v. a. To propose a premium for the performance of a task. The phrase originates in a custom that prevailed of throwing a bīṛā of betel into the midst of an assembly, in token of an invitation to undertake some difficu…

n. m. S. बेष्ट, Pr. veḍḍhaī he surrounds. 1. An enclosure; a yard; court; compound. 2. A raft; float. 3. A fleet of ships; navy. A raft which is floated by the Mahomedans in honor of Khuāja Khizar. Besides the anniversary beṛā offered to propitiate him, hundreds of smaller ones with lamps may be seen on Thursdays especially in the month of Bhādoṅ. Sakhī kā beṛā pār . Prov. The liberal man shall cross the Styx. 4. Army; troop; militia . 5. Association; company; society; party. beṛā pār lagānā, yā karnā , v. a. 1. To ferry over a raft or boat. 2. To remove difficulties; help one through a business; relieve from distress. beṛā pār honā , v. n. To succeed; gain one's end. 1. Maulā yār to beṛā pār . Prov. With God's helping hand you shall see land (heaven). 2. Ab ke wār meṅ beṛā pār hai . Prov. Saying. This throw I gain, my bark shall cross the main. 3. Dunyānajānisko, miyāṅ, daryā kī ye manj…
s. m. A betel leaf made up with a preparation of the areca nut, spices and chunam.

def: s.m. Brother ( = bīr, bīran).

s.m. A preparation of the areca nut with spices and chunam, &c. enveloped in a leaf of the betel plant (much used by the natives of India, and commonly presented by one to another in token of civility or affection. It is also given in confirmation of a pledge, promise, or betrothal; and, among the Rājpūts, is sometimes exchanged as a challenge); betel, pān (ten bīṛas = one gilauṛī); quid of tobacco; cheroot or cigar; — tie, fastening; thong tied to the hilt of a sword by which it is retained in the scabbard; sword-knot; hilt of a sword
more: s. m. 1. The same as S̱. بيرا bīrā, q. v. 2. A thong tied to the hilt of a sword by which it is retained in the scabbard, a sword knot. بيڙااٿهانا bīṛā uṭhānā, v. a. To undertake a business. بيڙاڐالنا bīṛā ḍālnā, To propose a premium for the performance of a task. (These expressions originate in a custom that prevailed of throwing a bīṛā of betel into the midst of an assembly, in token of proposal to any person to undertake some difficult affair then requisite to be performed; and, the person who took up the betel bound himself to perform the business in question).
subs: (-kā), To take up the gauntlet; to undertake to accomplish a business or affair; to take an enterprise upon oneself

(-kā), To propose a premium for the performance of a task (the custom was to throw a bīṛā into the midst of an assembly in token of an invitation to the undertaking of some difficult or hazardous enterprise, and the person who took up the bīṛā thereby bound himself to perform the task in question)
s.m. A bīṛā of seven betel leaves sent by the father of the bride to the bridegroom as a sign of betrothal.
ex: چین کا بیڑا اس وقت بحر الکاہل میں ہے۔
cīn kā beṛā is vaqt bahar-ul-kāhil mẽ hai.
China's fleet is currently in the Pacific Ocean.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑁂𑀟𑀕 (beḍaga), from Sanskrit बेडक (beḍaka), बेडा (beḍā). Cognate to Sindhi ٻِيڙِي (ḇīṛī).

def: s.m. Making; compiling, composing, composition, compilation; embellishing, embellishment.
v.t. To make, fabricate, form, compose, &c. (see biraćan).
s.m. Fame, reputation, honour, glory; eulogy, panegyric.

n. f. 1. Brotherhood; fraternity; clan; connection; relationship. Birādrī meṅ amīr garīb sab barabar . Prov. Saying. The members of the brotherhood are equal all, both great and small. 2. Relation; relative; kindred; kinsfolk; kinsman. 3. A community; society. 4. A band of musicians called tāshe-wāle. birādarī se khārij , Ped. for H. jāt-bāhar , adj. Outcast; one put out of caste. birādarī se khārij kar'nā , v. a. To put out of caste; excommunicate ( jāt se nikālnā ).
n. m. A younger brother ( chhoṭā bhāī ).
n. m. A step-brother; uterine brother.
n. m. A half brother.
n. m. Full brother; own brother; brother of whole blood.
n. m. Foster brother.
n. f. Fratricide.
n. m. Nephew; brother's son.
more: s. m. A brotherʼs son, a nephew.

def: n. f. Niece; brother's daughter.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference, disinclination, aversion; disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference, disinclination, aversion; disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
adj. & s.m. Void of passion or desire; entertaining aversion or dislike; — stoical person, stoic; ascetic.
s.m. Separation (from, -kā or -se), parting, absence (particularly of lovers); love-song describing the pain of separation
more: n. m. S. विरह to abandon. 1. Separation (of lovers) ( judāī ). See. بچهويا 2. A love song describing the pain of separation. Gāūṅ na; gāūṅ, birah gāūṅ . Prov. Sing I will not, or I'll sing of parted lovers' sorrow aches.
s. m. Separation, parting, absence, especially the separation of lovers.
subs: birah-kī mārī, adj. & s.m.f. Stricken with the pain of separation; one who is so stricken ( = birhan, and birogan, birogī).

def: (S. virah+agni), s.f. lit. 'The fire of separation'; the intense grief or sorrow which possesses a lover who is separated from his beloved.
s.f. A woman suffering the pangs of separation from the object of her affection; a female lover whose beloved one is absent ( = birogan).
more: adj. f. (A woman) suffering the pangs of absence in love, (a female lover) separated from the object of her affection.

def: adj. & s.m. Suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, love-sick; amorous, sensual; — one who is love-sick, &c.
s.f. = birahan, q.v. Sickness resulting from separation, love-sickness.
more: E. n. f. 1. An advance made for cultivation or manufacture, or for a contract. 2. A broom ( buhārī ).

def: s.m. Splendour, beauty ( = birāṭ, and see virāj).
adj. Splendid, brilliant, shining, handsome, gorgeous; holding the sceptre, sitting in state, presiding.
more: adj. S. बिराजमान from वि + राज् to shine forth. 1. Brilliant; shining; splendid; handsome. 2. Presiding; sitting; holding the sceptre.

def: v.t. To impart splendour or lustre to, to adorn, grace, beautify; — v.n. To shine, be splendid or conspicuous; to appear; to sit in state, sit majestically; to preside; to lord it, live at ease, enjoy oneself; to live in health, ease, content, or independence.
more: v. n. 1. To grace; adorn; set off ( sohnā ). 1. Kān kunḍal, sīs mukaṭ birāje . Song. Ṭhumrī . 2. Sīs mutyan kā sehrā birāje . Song. Banṛā . The nuptial wreath of pearls the head adorned. 2. To sit majestically; to preside. Ek samaë sab sahit samājā, Rāj-sabhā Raghurāj birājā . Rāmāyan. 3. To enjoy one's self; to live in health, ease, content. 4. To live; dwell. Nīl-kanṭh kīṛā bhake, mukhai birāje Rām, Khoṭ kapaṭ kyā dekhiye, darshan hī se kām . Dohā.
v. n. To be conspicuous or splendid; to enjoy oneʼs self; to live in health, ease, content and independence.

def: part. & s.m. Disinclined; inattentive; wearied, disgusted, alienated; — a kind of ascetic who has relinquished the world.
ex: اَچّھے کاموں سے بَرْکَتیں مِلْتی ہَیں۔
acche kāmõ se barkatẽ miltī ha͠i.
You receive blessings from doing good deeds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برکت (barakat), from Arabic بَرَكَة (baraka).

def: adj. & adv. Not close or compact, open in texture; thin, delicate, fine; loose, wide apart; apart, remote; rare, unfrequent, uncommon, scarce, wonderful; little, few; — rarely, seldom, unfrequently.

s.m. Cessation, rest, repose; stoppage, stop, pause; name of a small oblique stroke placed under a consonant to denote that it is quiescent, or that it has no vowel inherent or otherwise pronounced after it (syn. sukūn).
more: n. m. S. विराम from वि & रम् to rest. 1. Stop; pause; rest; period; full stop. 2. ( Gram .) A certain mark ( ् ) ( hal ), put under a consonant to indicate that it is not to be pronounced with the inherent vowel sound (A. sukūn ). In Prosody it is of two kinds, sentential and harmonic . The sentential pause occurs at the end of correspondent lines and is marked by the sign (I) half pause , (II) full pause . The harmonic pause or cœsura varies in different metres, and is marked by no sign. Kellogg's Grammar . 3. E. Rus. Restless; uneasy; ill ( beārām ).

def: adj. corr. of bīmār, q.v.
corr. of bīmār, and of be-ārām, q.q.v.
v.n. To stop, pause; to remain; to delay (cf. bilambnā).
more: v. n. To stop, to remain, to delay.

def: s.m. Brother ( = bīr, bīrā).
s.m. A kind of coarse, tough grass found covering uncultivated lands
adj. (f. -ī), Of, or relating to, or belonging to, another; another's; strange, foreign; extraneous, extrinsic (cf. P. bīrūnī).
more: adj. H. an another's. Belonging or relating to another ( parāyā ); strange; foreign; alien.
adj. 1. Strange, foreign. 2. Belonging to another, of another.

def: v.t. To vex, annoy, offend; to tease, mock, jibe, jeer.
adj. Ninety-two ( = bānwe, q.v.).
more: adj. Ninety two: see بانوي bānawe.

def: s.m. Name of Brahmā (also written birinć, birinći).
more: s. m. A name of Brahmā.

def: s.m. Relinquishing, sending away, dismissing, dismissal; throwing the image of a deity into holy water (as the concluding rite of a festival); finish, end, or conclusion of a religious ceremony; giving away, gift, donation.
more: n. m. S. विसर्ज्जन from सृज्ज् to throw. The finish, end, or conclusion of a religious ceremony; throwing the image of a deity into holy water as the concluding rite of a festival. Kathā bisarjan hot hai, suno, Bīr Hanumān, Rām, Lakshman, Jānkī sadā kareṅ kallyān . Tulsī Dās.

def: v.n. To be forgotten; to forget, lose the remembrance (of); to be unmindful (of).
more: v. n. S. विस्मरणं from वि neg., स्मृ to remember. To forget ( bhūlnā ); lose; to be effaced.
v. n. To forget, to be forgotten.

def: v.t. To cause to be forgotten; to make (one) forget; to efface, or dismiss, from the memory, to forget ( = bisrānā).
more: v. a. S. विस्मरणं from वि neg. स्मृ to remember. To forget; efface or dismiss from the memory. Aise gur par tan, man wārūṅ, Rām tajūṅ, aru gur na bisārūṅ . Bhajan. A priest so true that for him mind and body I would stake, Even Rām give up, but naught should make me e'er my priest forsake.
(trans. of بسرنا) v. a. To forget.

def: adj. = biswāsī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. biswāsin, -inī), Confiding, faithful, believing; honest, trusty; — truster, believer; confidant.
adj. = biswāsī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. biswāsin, -inī), Confiding, faithful, believing; honest, trusty; — truster, believer; confidant.

ety: S. विस्टत; or विस्तृत; cf. A. bisāt̤
def: s.f. lit. 'That which is spread out'; goods, wares, stock-in-trade; capital, substance, means, power; ability; significance, importance, moment, weight.
more: n. f. S. विस्तृत extended. 1. Ability; capability; strength; power. Is bachche kī kyā bisāt hai? 2. Capital; stock; estate; means. 3. Weight; difficulty; moment. ( Rare ). Is kām kī kyā bisāt hai? It's a very simple matter.
s. f. Means, capital stock, estate.

def: s.f. Anything that is spread out; surface, expanse, expansion; carpet; bedding; chess-cloth or chess-board, dice-board; — goods, wares, &c. ( = bisāt, q.v.).
more: n. f. بسط spread out. See بسات 1. Bedding; carpeting. 2. A chess-board; dice-board.
(from بسط) s. f. What is spread out; carpeting, bedding, a chess-board, dice-board, expanse, surface, expansion, &c. This word is, also, adopted for H. بسات bisāt, q. v. ¶ basāt̤, An extensive plain.

def: s.m. Vender of small wares (so called because he spreads out his wares); pedlar, huckster (syn. pherī-wālā, box-wālā, paikār, ḵẖụrda-farosh).
more: n. m. from H. bisānā to buy. (?) A small trader in miscellaneous goods; a pedlar.
One who sells every kind of things, a pedler, haberdasher.

def: s.m. = bisātī, q.v. s.m. Vender of small wares (so called because he spreads out his wares); pedlar, huckster (syn. pherī-wālā, box-wālā, paikār, ḵẖụrda-farosh).
s.f. = bisāhan, or bisāh, q.v. s.f. Buying, purchasing; incurring; thing bought, purchase.
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v. s.f. Smell of raw meat; fishy smell; rank or offensive smell, stench, stink, fetidness, rankness; coarseness, baseness, vulgarity.
s.m. = biswās, q.v. s.m. Trust, confidence, faith, belief, reliance, dependence
subs: biswās karnā (-kā; or -par), To put trust (in), to confide (in), rely (on), to trust, believe, credit
s.f. Destruction of confidence, breach of faith, violation of trust, treachery, treacherous friendship; — biswās-ghātī, s.m. One who betrays confidence, traitor, treacherous friend.

def: s.m. Call made to a cat; puss-puss.
v.n. To give forth a sound as in fermentation or putrefaction; to be in a state of fermentation, or putrefaction; to ferment ( = baj-bajānā); to smart, tingle.
more: v. n. H. bās smell. 1. W. To yield a sound as in the fermentation of putrifying vegetables or of vinous liquors; to ferment. 2. E. To smart; tingle; burn.
v. n. To yield a sound as in the fermentation of putrefying vegetables or of vinous liquors. See بج بجانا.

def: s.m. = biśesh, q.v.
s.m. = biśesh, q.v.

n. m. S. विशेष from वि and शिष् to distinguish. 1. Difference; unlikeness; diversity; distinction. Shokhī, sharārat, makr o fan, sab kā bisekhā hai yahāṅ, Jo jo dikhāyā aur ko woh āp bhī dekhā yahāṅ . Nazīr. Insult and hurt, deceit and fraud, All have fit retribution known, Whatever one to others showed, Here to himself too has been shown. 2. Special property; peculiarity; habit ( bān ). In nainan kā yehī bisekh, Yeh bhī dekhā, woh bhī dekh . Prov. These eyes endowed are with this special bent, Now this one seen, on that too now intent. 3. The greater number; the majority. 4. ( In law ). A special rule or distinction. 5. ( In Med .) A change for the better. bisekh', basekh , adj. 1. Special; peculiar; particular; distinctive. 2. Superior; prime; excellent. bisekh, yā bashesh kar-ke , adv. 1. Particularly; specially. 2. (Wom.) About; on the point of. Marne basekh paṛā hai .
more: s. m. 1. Particular circumstance. 2. adj. Peculiar, special, particular, abundant. 3. adv. Particlarly, specially See بشيش.

def: s.m. Name of a powerful tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. Abundance = biśeshatā, q.v. s.f. Peculiarity, speciality; abundance, plenty.
s.m. = biśesh, q.v.

s.m. Difference, distinction, individuality, characteristic difference, peculiar mark, particular circumstance, special property, speciality, peculiarity, attribute; distinction, peculiar merit, excellence, superiority; sort, kind, manner; (in Gram.) a defined or qualified substantive (syn. mauṣūf); (in Law), special rule or distinction; (in Med.) change for the better; — adj. Distinguished, definite, particular, special, peculiar, distinctive; certain, choice, excellent, superior; abundant, excessive; — adv. Especially, particularly, specially
subs: biśesh-kar-ke, adv. More especially, particularly, par excellence.

def: adj. & s.m. Discriminative, distinctive, attributive; — distinguishing mark, distinction, attribute, characteristic, epithet; (in Gram.), the qualifying word, adjective, adverb ( = kriyā-viśeshan).
s.f. Peculiarity, speciality; abundance, plenty.
s.m. Poison, &c. = bis, q.v.
more: s. m. Poison. See بس bis.
subs: s.f. lit. 'Poison-damsel'; a female who has been fed on poisons from her infancy. (Such damsels, it is said, were formerly sent by Indian princes as presents to those whom they wished to destroy, as the poisonous constitution of the girl could immediately infect and kill an unwary lover.)

def: adj. Venomous (as reptiles).

n. m. 1. An object of sense; the senses ( indrī ); the faculty of perceiving; perception; the object perceived; the object; the exterior world 2. Worldly or sensual enjoyment; the pleasures of sense. 3. Lust; heat; sexual intercourse; sensuality. 4. Subject; topic; subject matter. 5. Function; office; work. 6. Affair; transaction; business. bishaë , adv. In respect of; respecting; about.
adj. = bisahā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Busied with worldly matters; addicted to worldly pleasures; worldly, sensual, carnal; — an epithet of Kām-deʼo, the Indian Cupid; anyone devoted to sensual or worldly objects; sensualist, voluptuary, debauchee, rake ( = bisnī, q.v.).
adj. = bisailā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous (syn. zahrīlā); — venomous reptile; snake; phlegmon or boil on the hand (bisahrī).

adj. Uneven, rough, difficult of access; unequal, irregular; not even, odd (number); difficult, hard; disagreeable, painful, troublesome, vexatious; odd, unusual, unequalled (in a bad sense); intermittent, inconstant; bad, adverse, unpropitious
more: adj. Unequalled (in a bad sense), difficult, bad, afflictive. بشمجر bisham-jar (or bish̤am-jwar, विषमज्वर ) s. m. An inflammatory fever, a high fever.
subs: bisham-jwar, s.m. Irregular fever, irregularly remittent fever
s.m. Unequal four-sided figure, trapezium, quadrilateral
adj. Unequal angled; — bisham-koṇ āyat, s.m. Parallelogram; rhomboid; — bisham-koṇ sam-ćatur-bhūj, s.m. Rhombus.

def: A particular song in the name of Vish̤ṇu.

s.m. Object of sense; object of concern or attention, worldly object or aim; object, pursuit, affair, concern, business, transaction; matter, topic, subject, region, sense; worldly or sensual enjoyment, sensuality, lust; — postp. In the matter (of, -ke), in respect (of), respecting, concerning, about ( = bābat)
subs: In this matter; in this respect.

def: adj. & s.m. Busied with worldly matters; addicted to worldly pleasures; worldly, sensual, carnal; — an epithet of Kām-deʼo, the Indian Cupid; anyone devoted to sensual or worldly objects; sensualist, voluptuary, debauchee, rake ( = bisnī, q.v.).
See بسيکهـ bisekh̤.
n. m. See بسيک 1. Superiority; excellence. 2. ( Gram .) A qualified noun (A. mausūj ). bishesh ,' adj. Excessive. See adhik. bishesh kar-ke , adv. More especially.
adj. = bisyar; bisailā, q.q.v. adj. Much, many, very, abundant.
s.m. Name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects; a member of that sect; a worshipper of Vishnu.
s.m. = bishnawī, q.v. s.m. Name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects; a member of that sect; a worshipper of Vishnu.
more: n. m. S. विषयिन् from विषि to attach. 1. An epithet of Kām-deo , the god of love; Cupid. 2. Any one given up to sensual or worldly objects; a sensualist; debauchee. 3. A lover; paramour; customer. Be-zar bishnī, bhaṛwe barābar . Prov. A penniless customer is no better than a pimp.
s. m. A rake, &c. See بسني bisnī.

def: s.m. = bishayī and bisnī, q.q.v. adj. & s.m. Busied with worldly matters; addicted to worldly pleasures; worldly, sensual, carnal; — an epithet of Kām-deʼo, the Indian Cupid; anyone devoted to sensual or worldly objects; sensualist, voluptuary, debauchee, rake ( = bisnī, q.v.).
n. m. S. वैष्णव. A worshipper of Vishnu.
s.m. A Hindū god, the Deity in the character of Preserver
more: n. m. S. विष्णु One of the Hindu triad, and the husband of Lakshmī; the Preserver. bishn-pad , n. m. A hymn to Vishnu. bishn prīt , n. f. Land given for religious purposes. bishan-devā , n. m. A sect of mendicants who beg in the name of Vishnu.
n. prop. (in Hindū mythology) The deity in the character of Preserver.
subs: (S. vishṇu+padya), s.m. A particular song in the name of Vishnu
s.f. Land granted rent-free to Brāhmans in honour of Vishnu, or to maintain his worship
s.m. The holder of a bishn-prīt, or grant of land for religious purposes
s.m. A sect of mendicants who beg in the name of Vishnu.

def: s.m. A provincial term (in Kamāʼūn) for a kind of taʻalluqdār whose office is in the gift of government.
s.f. Ordure, excrement, filth, dirt.
Māṛ. n. f. Ordure; filth; dirt.
s.f. Rain.
s.f. Strip of cloth (to cover the privities with); shred, scrap.

Tir. n. f. S. वेष्ट् to wrap round. A shred; scrap; a strip of the laṅgoṭī , about a span only. Libṛī, batānā chor sab le līyā, ek bishṭī de dīyā . E. The thieves my turban took, my garments all beside, A shred they left me just my nakedness to hide.
s.f. = bīsī, q.v.
s.f. A score; a certain measure of grain; a land-measure equivalent to twenty nalīs.
more: n. f. 1. A score ( koṛī ); twenty. 2. A measure of grain. Wohī tīn bīsī, wohī sāṭh . Prov. Three score and sixty are the same. (Six of one and half a dozen of the other). 3. A land measure of 20 nalīs ( biswā ).
s. f. 1. A measure of grain. 2. adj. (for विंशति ) Twenty, a score.

def: s.m. Arrow, iron crow.
s.m. = biswa-karmā, q.v.s.v. biswa. see بسوه biswa.

n. m. 1. A medicinal plant; Trianthemā pentandrā . It spreads over the ground, and forms a circle of nearly a yard in diameter. 2. An animal of the lizard kind, said to be venomous. bis kī gāṅṭh, bis kī khān, puṛyā, yā poṭ , A viper; a demon; a villain of the deepest dye, hence a mischievous person; a little devil. 1. Mīṭhī bātoṅ pe na jā bis kī hai woh gāṅṭh, muā, Kyā kahūṅ, us se jo sadme mujhe, guīyāṅ, pahuṅhche! Wom. Jān Sāhab. Put nor your trust in honeyed words which poison's essence are, Such strokes as I have borne, my dear, death take the wretch nor spare! 2. Baṅgāle mat jāwe, pī, jahāṅ bis kī khān, Nār wahāṅ kī popnī, nāhak jāëgī jān . E. Play. Chaubolā . To Bengal go not, love, 'tis poison's deadly quarry true, The women fatal sorcerers are, in vain your end you 'll rue. bis milā'nā , v. a. lit . To mix poison. To spoil; corrupt. Maiṅ ne kyā bis milā dīyā thā? Prov. Saying I mixed no poison in your cup.
s.m. See s.v. bis. s.m. Poison, venom, bane ( = zahr); the vegetable poison Aconitum ferox (syn. baćnāg); (met.), anything bitter, disagreeable, spiteful, &c.
more: s. m. The name of a medicinal plant. (Trianthema pentandra).
subs: v.n. lit. 'To vomit venom'; to say venomous or spiteful things (of), speak ill (of), malign, revile, sneer at; to disburden the mind or the conscience; to have (one's) say; to take revenge
adj. & s.m. Poisonous; a venomous serpent; snake
v.n. To swallow or take poison

bis-khān, bis-gāṅṭh, s.m.f. Viper, mischievous person, devil, demon
(-meṅ), To mingle poison (with), to poison, embitter, corrupt, vitiate, spoil

def: v.t. To break to pieces, shatter, destroy (a word peculiar to Hindū women).
more: v. a. Hin. Wom. To break; destroy; crack; smash. Nath bismāūṅ, hāth kī churyāṅ, Dekh pīyā kī lāsh lageṅ jaise chhuryāṅ . Play. Chaubolā . My nose-ring I will break and all my bangles too, My husband's corpse to see's a knife that cuts me through.

def: s.m. Name of a plant.
more: s. m. The name of a bush.

def: s.m. Astonishment, amazement, surprise, perplexity, bewilderment, dismay
adj. Astonished, taken by surprise, amazed, disconcerted, perplexed, bewildered, dismayed, afraid.
adj. Sacrificed, slaughtered
more: adj. Sacrificed; an animal sacrificed. بسمل کرنا bismil karnā, To sacrifice. بسمل گاه bismil-gāh, s. f. Place of sacrifice.
subs: v.t. To sacrifice; to slaughter (an animal)

def: adj. For sacrifice; for slaughter.
intj. God be with you! God speed you! very good! very well! all right!
more: Mah. Cor. of ba-ism-i-Allāh, lit . In the name of God. 1. A phrase generally used by pious Mohamedans in the beginning of all actions or lessons, and especially before eating. 1. Pahle hī bismillah galat . Prov. Wrong at the outset. 2. Har niwāle bismillāh . Prov. At every mouthful it's "please God, I will." ☞ Said to one who is always ready to take all he can get, but very unwilling to work. 2. A ceremony performed at a Persian school ( maktab ) when a new boy is set to read for the first time. bismillah ! intj. Fac. bis milā , mix poison (in the cup). 1. Heaven preserve us! God bless you! Har qadam pe bismillāh . Mah. At every step it's 'God be with you'. (Fac.). Bāt bāt meṅ bis milāte haiṅ . Hin. At every turn you poison find. 2. Very good! very well! all right! ready! aye ready! Bismīllah! Mah. Ans. Bis milāne-wāle kā satyānās . Hin. bismil-lāhir-rahmān-nir-rahīm . In the name of God…

In the name of God, or بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم bismi-l-lāhi-r-rahmāni-r-raḥīm, In the name of God the most merciful. (An invocation generally used by Muhammadans at the beginning of all actions and works). بسم الله کرنا bismi-l-lāh karnā, To begin, to commence. بسم الله هوا bismi-l-lāh hūʼā, Said of a child who has just commenced learning to read.
subs: In the name of God the Merciful, the Compassionate
v.n. To pronounce the formula 'in the name of God,' at the beginning of a work, etc.; (hence) to begin, commence; to fall to (eating), set to work; to slaughter (an animal)
v.n. To abide under the dome of security, live in peace and safety; to retire from the world, lead a life of seclusion
'A beginning has been made at his lessons' (said of a child who has just commenced learning to read).

def: part. Amazed, &c. = bismayī, q.v. adj. Astonished, taken by surprise, amazed, disconcerted, perplexed, bewildered, dismayed, afraid.

adj. & s.m. Vicious, dissolute, addicted to any kind of vice or evil practice; delicate, nice; showy in dress (syn. sughaṛ); — dissolute man, voluptuary, libertine, debauchee, rake, roué, lecher (cf. bishnī).
more: 1. s. m. A rake, a debauchee, a lecher. 2. adj. Delicate, nice, showy in dress.

def: adj. Free from grief or sorrow, happy.
s.m. Twenty days of an intercalary month (for which it is the custom among bohrās or money-lenders to cut off the interest); the interest so cut off.
adj. Forgetful, oblivious; negligent.

def: s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, cessation from labour or fatigue; pause, stop, point in writing
more: n. m. S. विश्राम from वि apart, श्रम् to labour. 1. Rest; repose; ease; comfort. 1. Rām kāj kīne binā, mohe kahāṅ bisrām, Daë asīs Sursā chalī, harakh chale Hanūmān . Rāmāyan. 2. Tan takyā, aur man bisrām, Jahāṅ paṛ rahe, vahīṅ ārām . Prov. The body is a hermit's cell, the mind the tenant is, Where'er it chance to lie there rest that comes to all is his. 2. A pause; a stop; a point in writing. bisrām lenā, yā kar'nā , v. n. To rest; take rest; repose; lie down; sleep. Ham pardesī pāohne, ān kīyo bisrām, Bhor bhaë uṭh jāëṅge, baso tihāro gām! Dohā. A stranger and a guest I came here and found rest, At dawn I'll go, your village be for ever blest!
s. m. Rest, ease, repose, pause, a stop, a point in writing. بسرام لينا bisrām lenā, To rest, to repose, to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faḳīrs ).
subs: bisrām lenā, v.n. To rest, repose, &c.; to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faqīrs).

def: v.t. To cause to forget; to forget ( = bisārnā), to efface from the memory; to distract, beguile; to mislead (syn. bhulānā; bahkānā).
more: v. a. To efface from the memory; to distract (the senses.) 1. Aisī bansī bajāī, morī sudh bisrāī . Song. 2. Kab se rājā īshar bhaë, Kodoṅ ke din bīsar gaë? Prov. Since when a mighty king grown to, your altered lot, The days you lived on porridge meal now all forgot.
(causal of بسرنا) v. n. To forget, to cause to forget, to mislead.

def: part. Rested, reposed.
part. adj. = bisrānt, q.v. part. Rested, reposed.

def: s.m. A provincial term (in Kamāʼūn) for a kind of taʻalluqdār whose office is in the gift of government.
adj. Twenty.
more: (for بيست) Twenty. bast, part. (in compos.) Bound, shut. bust, s. A rose-bower or a place abounding with sweet-smelling fruits.

def: s.m. Spreading out; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development, amplification, detail, prolixity, copiousness, abundance; (in Geom.) diameter of a circle
more: n. m. S. विस्तार from वि and स्मृ spread. 1. Extension; expansion; spread ( phailāo ); diffusion; enlargement; amplification; size. 1. Sat yojan samudr bistārā, Gayo Pawan-sut tāke pārā . Rāmāyan. 2. Ādhī kīch, tihāī jal, daswāṅ ans siwār, Bāwan gaz bāhar rahī, silā kitek bistār? Math. Problem. Ans. 780. 2. Abundance; copiousness; prolixity. 3. Amplification; detail; explanation; exposition. 4. ( Math .) The diameter of a circle ( qutr ).
s. m. Spreading out, abundance, copiousness, prolixity, diffusion.
subs: (-kā), To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c.

def: s.m. Bedding, mattress (cf. bistarā); carpet; bed, bolster, pillow.
more: n. m. S. स्तृ to spread. 1. A bedding ( bichhonā ); mattress; carpeting. 2. The abode or resting place of a faqīr. Kafan hamārī gūdṛī, gor hamāro gāoṅ, Lahad hamārā bistārā, khāk hamaro nāoṅ . Mah. Dohā. The winding sheet my covering, and the grave my hame, The washing board my bedding is, and dust my name. 3. A plant; Callicarpa Americanā. bistrā lagā'nā, bichhānā, yā jamānā , v. n. To spread a carpet; make the bed.
s. m. 1. Bedding, carpeting. 2. A bed, &c.

def: s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
more: s. m. 1. A bed, &c. peculiar to faḳīrs. 2. The abode of a faḳīr.
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: adj. Extending, large, ample, diffuse.

v.t. To spread, extend, &c. = bistār karnā, q.v.s.v. bistār. s.m. Spreading out; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development, amplification, detail, prolixity, copiousness, abundance; (in Geom.) diameter of a circle
more: v. a. To spread out; expand; enlarge; lengthen ( baṛhānā ).
v. a. To spread out, to extend, to diffuse.
subs: (-kā), To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c.

def: s.f. The house-lizard (sy. n ćhipkalī; pallī).
s.f. The house-lizard (syn. ćhipkalī; pallī).
v.n. To sob, &c. = bisūrnā, q.v.
v.n. To put on a crying face, to prepare to weep; to cry slowly; to sob; to pull a long face.
more: v. n. Pr. visūra to be distressed. 1. To look as if about to weep; to be ready to cry. Nā mere paṅkh, na pāoṅ bal, maiṅ apaṅkh, pīyā dūr, Uṛ na sakūṅ, gir gir paṛūṅ, rahūṅ bisūr bisūr . No wings have I, nor strength of limb, Wingless, my husband far from me, I cannot fly, I drop and fall, And sob alone with misery. 2. To make a wry face; to be sulky. Pissū se, machchharoṅ se rove, koī bisūre . Nazīr. Fleas and musquitoes do the rains bring out, Some crying and some sobbing are about.
v. n. To cry slowly, to sob.

def: see بسوه biswa.
more: s. m. The universe, the world.

ety: Ap. Prk. वीसवँओ = Prk. वीसमओ = S. विंशम+कः
def: s.m. Twentieth part; a measure of land, the twentieth part of a bīghā
more: s. m. A land measure, the twentieth part of a بيگها bīghā. See بسوه.
subs: s.m., biswāṅsī, s.f. (i.e. biswā+ansa = S. anśa+kah), Twentieth part of a biswā
s.m. Collecting, or collection, by the biswā
s.m. Holder of a share or shares in a coparcenary village; — biswā-dārī, s.f. Coparcenary holding or tenure; tenure of independent village communities under a superior taʻallug-dār.

def: s.m. = biswā, q.v. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. m. H. bīs twenty. The twentieth part of a bīgah ; a land measure; a share of land. biswah-dār , n. m. The holder of a share or shares in a coparcenary village. bīs bisve ; Poet. bisve bīs , adv. lit . All the twenty biswās (in a bīgah ). Certainly; surely; no doubt; necessarily. Hon-hār miṭtī nahīṅ, hove bisve bīs . Prov. What is to be will not rub out, but surely be. das bisweh , n. m. Ten biswās or half a bīgah , hence a half share.
s. m. The twentieth part (particularly, of a بيگها bīghā ). See کٿها kaṭṭhā.

def: adj. & s.m. All, every, every one, entire, whole, universal; — whole world, all creation, universe
subs: s.m. lit. 'All-maker'; name of the artificer or architect of the gods, the Vulcan of Hindu mythology; the god of artisans (especially of carpenters); — an astute or crafty person.

def: s.m. See s.v. biswa. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. m. S. विश्वकर्म्मा A son of Brahma; a saint. 1. An artificer of the gods; the Indian Vulcan; the god of carpenters. 2. A crafty or mischievous person.
s. m. The artificer of the gods, the Indian vulcan.

def: adj. Twentieth
subs: adv. In the twentieth place, twentiethly.

def: adj. S. बिंश, Pr. bīso . Twentieth. bīsyoṅ, bīsoṅ , adj. Numberless; countless ( an-ginat ).
s.f. See s.v. biswā. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. f. The twentieth part of a Biswā .

def: s.m. (?), Mixture of spices and aromatic seeds powdered and ready for use in curry or pickle; curry-powder.
more: n. bīs twenty. A mixture of spices, etc. used in curries and pickles.

def: s.m. Trust, confidence, faith, belief, reliance, dependence
more: Hin. n. m. S. विश्वास, Pr. vissāso, vīsāso . 1. Faith; belief; confidence; trust; reliance; dependance. 2. (A. vasvās ). Doubt; suspicion. Illit. biswās karnā yā rakhnā , Hin. v. n. To believe; credit; rely upon; confide. Rām nām kahte raho, mat kar tan biswās . Ratnāvlī. On Rām's name ever call, nor of the body aught take thought at all. biswās-ghāt , n. f. Taking advantage of the confidence of others; a treacherous friendship ( yār-mārī ). biswās-ghātī , n. m. A treacherous friend ( yār-mār ); one who seeks to take advantage of the confidence placed in him.

s. m. Trust, confidence, faith. بسواس گهات biswās-ghāt, s. f. Taking advantage of the confidence of others, treacherous friendship. بسواسگهاتي biswās-ghāt-ī, s. m. A treacherous friend, one who seeks to take advantage of the confidence placed in him.
subs: biswās karnā (-kā; or -par), To put trust (in), to confide (in), rely (on), to trust, believe, credit
s.f. Destruction of confidence, breach of faith, violation of trust, treachery, treacherous friendship; — biswās-ghātī, s.m. One who betrays confidence, traitor, treacherous friend.

def: s.m. Corr. of A. waswās, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. biswāsin, -inī), Confiding, faithful, believing; honest, trusty; — truster, believer; confidant.
adj. Of or relating to the world, worldly, belonging to the universe.
more: n. f. Alienation of land on the payment of fines in advance.
adj. Worldly, relating to the world.

def: s.f. Alienation of land on low jamaʻs on the payment of fines in advance.
